published.text:
Badge: Vancouver, British Columbia
Heading: AI Automation Consultant for Vancouver Businesses
Body: Your team is spending hours on work that should run itself. Lead follow-up, scheduling, data entry, CRM updates. We build AI-powered automations that handle it so your people focus on revenue.
Sub: Below: what we do, who we work with, and how to tell if automation consulting is the right move for your business.
CTA: Book a 30-Minute Call | All Guides

### What we automate for Vancouver businesses
Intro: Every business is different, but the bottlenecks tend to fall into the same categories.
- Lead follow-up and response: Instant SMS and email responses to every inquiry. Multi-touch follow-up sequences that run until the lead books or opts out.
- Appointment scheduling: AI-powered booking that qualifies leads, checks availability, and confirms appointments without your team touching a thing.
- CRM cleanup and automation: Leads logged, tagged, and enrolled in the right sequences automatically.
- Operations and internal workflows: Invoice generation, reporting, inventory updates, team notifications.
- AI voice agents: 24/7 phone answering for booking, qualification, and routing.
- Multi-location coordination: Systems that keep scheduling, lead routing, and reporting consistent across all locations.

### Vancouver industries we work with
Intro: Vancouver's economy runs on services, tech, real estate, and professional firms.
- Real estate teams and brokerages: Lead qualification, showing coordination, drip campaigns, offer follow-up
- Dental and medical clinics: Patient booking, intake, reminders, follow-up
- HVAC, plumbing, and electrical: Estimate requests, dispatch, seasonal demand management
- Law firms and accounting practices: Consultation booking, document intake, client follow-up
- Property management companies: Tenant communication, maintenance triage, lease renewals
- Fitness, wellness, and coaching: Class booking, membership management, retention sequences

### How we work
1. 30-minute discovery call: Look at current operations, identify biggest bottlenecks, figure out where automation will have the most impact.
2. Scope and fixed quote: Define exactly what gets built, what it connects to, what the deliverable looks like. Fixed price and timeline before work starts.
3. Build and test: Build automation, test with real data, make sure it works with existing tools.
4. Launch and support: System goes live. Monitor for first two weeks. Optional ongoing maintenance retainer.

### FAQ
- Do I need to be in Vancouver to work with you? No. We work remotely with businesses across North America. We are based in Alberta and serve clients across Canada, including Vancouver and the rest of British Columbia.
- What size business is this for? Most clients are small to mid-size businesses with 5 to 100 employees. Sweet spot is $500K to $10M in annual revenue.
- How long does a typical automation project take? Focused automation: 1 to 3 weeks. Larger system: 3 to 6 weeks.
- What tools and platforms do you work with? n8n, Make, custom API integrations. OpenAI, Anthropic, Vapi, Bland for AI. GoHighLevel, HubSpot, Salesforce for CRMs.
- What if I am not sure what to automate first? That is exactly what the initial call is for. Most businesses have 2 to 3 obvious automation opportunities once someone maps out the workflows.

edits.description: Remote consultant positioning: honest about Alberta base, serving Vancouver remotely
References Vancouver economy naturally (real estate, property management, tech)
Same direct, practical tone as other geo pages
Fixed pricing emphasized as trust signal
Adjacent proof only: case studies from other markets, no fake Vancouver-specific claims
